title: Ramiel
wikitext:
[]: Ramiel             Quote Post by Zuggy » Sat Sep 10, 2005 10:27 am Here we go again. :roll: Some thoughts and theories on Ramiel: ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Shape: Ramielâ€™s shape is an Octahedron. Type: Platonic Solid Number of Faces: 8 Number of Vertices: 6 Number of Edges: 12 If you â€˜unfoldâ€™ an octahedron it can fit comfortably within parts of the Seporith Tree of Life. {Hereâ€™s a link to a picture of an unfolded octahedron. See it?} {If you mess around with the Tree of Life some more you get this.} If you view Ramiel from a 45 degree angle it has an exact outline of a hexagon. The same shape as an AT field. ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- Particle Weapon: The Inner Torus Reactor interested me, so I poked about. The source of the angels particle weapon doesnâ€™t appear to be from its S^2 engine (unless this engine is a Torus reactor for the other angels.) Torus reactors have already been made, they are also called Tokamak reactors. {A link to an example of one of these reactors, which is also called JET.} So basically to charge up its weapon, it rotates its magnetic/A.T field and compresses the space its reactor and somehow generates and then projects some plasma energy. Simple. The current reactors we have cant work continuously, because of the massive stress, so this angel is one tough fuck to cope with a continuous energy discharge like that. Or something like that :) You could use the above paragraph to explain how Sachiel self-destructed, simply overburden this reactor and youâ€™ll end up with a nasty nuclear explosion. Probably. Although I could be completely wrong with all of this, I really simplified most of the above ; ) ------------------------------------------------------------------------------------- My Questions: It is the first angel we see in the series that has no core visible. Why? After it is destroyed we see NERV â€˜dismantlingâ€™ the angel. The inside of it looks to have a dense framework or skeleton. How come we can see straight through it in the earlier episode? The drill it uses to break through the Geo-Front is interesting, after the â€˜lasersâ€™ at the tip have stopped we can clearly see a mechanical structure underneath. With what looks like a metallic surface and screwsâ€¦ Any thoughts on whether or not this angel is some sort of machine? Ramiel is one of the more interesting angels. Maybe if we figure out some stuff here it'll be germane to other aspects of Evangelion. 8)

[]:             Quote Post by akda1ndaonly1 » Sat Sep 10, 2005 4:33 pm Zugzwang wrote: The drill it uses to break through the Geo-Front is interesting, after the â€˜lasersâ€™ at the tip have stopped we can clearly see a mechanical structure underneath.

[]:             Quote Post by Space Penis » Sat Sep 10, 2005 6:48 pm It is the first angel we see in the series that has no core visible. Why?

[]:             Quote Post by Mr. Tines » Sun Sep 11, 2005 6:03 am The only mention of the core in the LTP is Ritsuko's mission instructions to Shinji: [code:1]Ritsuko:This arrangement is due to the fact that Shinji's synchronization ratio is higher and we need more precise operation this time. Since the positron beam is influenced by the magnetic field, gravity field and the revolution of the Earth, it will not fire in a straight line. Don't forget to adjust for that error--you must hit the core precisely.[/code:1]

[]:             Quote Post by Hexon.Arq » Sun Sep 11, 2005 7:53 pm It is the first angel we see in the series that has no core visible. Why?

[]: Re: Ramiel             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Sep 14, 2005 8:35 am Zugzwang wrote: You could use the above paragraph to explain how Sachiel self-destructed, simply overburden this reactor and youâ€™ll end up with a nasty nuclear explosion. Probably.

[]: Re: Ramiel             Quote Post by Space Penis » Wed Sep 14, 2005 9:47 am Reichu wrote: Certainly not. Ramiel's true form, like all Angels, is that of a humanoid, and the floating octahedron is just something she chose to become.

[]: Re: Ramiel             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Sep 14, 2005 10:00 am Space Penis wrote: Hey, whatnow? Where's this come from?

[]: Re: Ramiel             Quote Post by Space Penis » Wed Sep 14, 2005 10:12 am Reichu wrote: Space Penis wrote: Hey, whatnow? Where's this come from?

[]: Re: Ramiel             Quote Post by Reichu » Wed Sep 14, 2005 11:21 am Space Penis wrote: Could you be a bit more, uh, specific?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Wed Sep 14, 2005 3:47 pm So what about Iruel?

[]:             Quote Post by Soluzar » Wed Sep 14, 2005 6:08 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: So what about Iruel?

[]:             Quote Post by akda1ndaonly1 » Wed Sep 14, 2005 7:00 pm Soluzar wrote: ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: So what about Iruel?

[]:             Quote Post by Defectron » Wed Sep 14, 2005 9:08 pm Iruel seems to be a nanotech based angel, a lot of people trying to get working nanotech have tried using organic things like human dna as parts in the nanomachines, so there's really no reason why Iruel wouldnt be organic. Although I'm wondering about what sort of core that sort of angel would have? Would each nanite have a mini core? As for ramial, you can't deny much of that angel seems to be very inorganic even if it did start off organic, especially that drill!

[]:             Quote Post by Oblivious » Wed Sep 14, 2005 9:15 pm Defectron wrote: As for ramial, you can't deny much of that angel seems to be very inorganic even if it did start off organic, especially that drill!

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Sep 15, 2005 3:11 am Defectron wrote: Iruel seems to be a nanotech based angel, a lot of people trying to get working nanotech have tried using organic things like human dna as parts in the nanomachines, so there's really no reason why Iruel wouldnt be organic. Although I'm wondering about what sort of core that sort of angel would have? Would each nanite have a mini core?

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Thu Sep 15, 2005 1:45 pm Reichu wrote: Although I shoudl mention, an entity like Ireul seems to support Shin-seiki's Quantum Magic idea -- that souls in NGE have weird properties that would enable, say, Ireul here to have one soul occuring in all of these nanites at once. Brings Israfel to mind. I've always wondered: Does Israfel have one soul or two? I've tried to reason which idea is more probable - one soul occuring in two places at once when ve splits, or two souls coexisting in Israfel's singular form.

[]:             Quote Post by Zuggy » Thu Sep 15, 2005 2:14 pm O.M.F. wrote: Soul severability?

[]:             Quote Post by Reichu » Thu Sep 15, 2005 5:58 pm ObsessiveMathsFreak wrote: Soul severability? I'm not happy with the theory personally, but perhaps these two angels could be used as evidence.

[]:             Quote Post by ObsessiveMathsFreak » Fri Sep 16, 2005 12:59 pm On the subject of Rei's core. Are we sure she doesn't have one? What about Kaworu? What was powering both of their AT Fields?
